text: DeepSeek recently released DeepSeek-R1 a fully open-source model that is quite far from the ChatGPT output, and what we see so far is incredibly promising for the AI community. This paper goes into great detail about the DeepSeek-R1 model structure and highlights its differences compared to other models like ChatGPT. While DeepSeek-R1 uses mostly rule-based reinforcement learning (RL), it forgoes the preliminary supervised fine-tuning (SFT) phase to achieve superior performance in complex reasoning tasks, and also gets a fine tuning from a precise reward modeling system, utilizing a multi-stage training process that includes “cold-start” data before entering into the RL phase. It also highlights the competition between DeepSeek and other models like ChatGPT in various tasks, and DeepSeek’s dominance in technical and logical tasks with its transparent results due to the open-source nature of DeepSeek, while ChatGPT is still winning in general, in creative applications, and in conversational proficiency. Although there are many breakthroughs shown by DeepSeek-R1 and ChatGPT, the field is still long way to go to reach the AI promising goals and dreams, with many challenges need to be solved including the quality of the data, the mystery of the so called “black box” nature of the deep neural networks, privacy and social challenges as the replacement of human work by AI which is becoming more evident and visible in our everyday lives. With the many technological advancements we are expecting to reach in the AI field in the near future, including multi-modal capabilities and the high-level AI algorithms to manage the complexity of the large data-sets, which will definitely change the user experience of AI systems in a large number of applications and for a large group of users, and maybe, hopefully could pave the way to achieve the AI promised intelligence for all and not only the privileged few.
explanation: The passage contains promotional, high-level claims with few concrete details or verifiable references, a pattern common in AI-generated marketing prose. The wording is polished and slightly generic, including repeated buzzwords and speculative comparisons.
